description: A V8 sandbox bypass vulnerability exists in the JSToWasmWrapperHelper builtin (src/builtins/js-to-wasm.tq). When a JavaScript-to-WebAssembly call wrapper returns, it must pop all arguments from the stack. To calculate the pop count, it reads `target.shared_function_info.length` (an int16 field on the SharedFunctionInfo heap object) and uses this as the number of arguments to pop via `PopAndReturn(popCount + 1, result)`. Because SharedFunctionInfo is a heap object residing inside the V8 sandbox, an attacker who has already gained sandbox-level write access can corrupt the `length` field to an arbitrarily large value. This causes `PopAndReturn` to advance the stack pointer far beyond the current frame boundary, redirecting execution to attacker-controlled memory and enabling a full sandbox escape (RIP hijacking).

fixes.description: Minimal compatibility fix for this checkout: use the trusted Wasm signature parameter count for JSToWasmWrapper stack popping instead of mutable SharedFunctionInfo::length.
fixes.evidence_text: Report identifies JSToWasmWrapper reading target.shared_function_info.length for PopAndReturn as the exploitable mutable field.
